description:
secretx-aws-kms/src/lib.rs:326-338 implements SecretStore for AwsKmsBackend, returning SecretError::Backend{source: 'aws-kms is a signing-only backend'} on every call.

The crate only registers as SigningBackendRegistration (line 340), and the umbrella crate secretx/src/lib.rs:103-108 explicitly returns InvalidUri 'aws-kms is a signing-only backend; use from_signing_uri instead' before any SecretStore method on AwsKmsBackend could be reached. This is also covered by from_uri_aws_kms_signing_only_returns_invalid_uri test.

Maintenance consequence:
  - The impl is dead code through the public API. A user who reaches it is using AwsKmsBackend directly (skipping from_uri), and the runtime error message they see ('aws-kms is a signing-only backend; use SigningBackend') is not the same as the umbrella one.
  - More importantly, this is a runtime-deferred type-level constraint: the type system already says aws-kms is signing-only (it does not implement WritableSecretStore), so the SecretStore impl is the only thing forcing the runtime stub.

Recommendation: delete the SecretStore impl. SigningBackend does not require SecretStore. If something later truly needs both (which would be a design error for HSM-backed keys), reintroduce with a clear doc comment explaining why the runtime error is unavoidable.

If kept: at minimum, document at the impl site why it exists despite being unregistered, so a future maintainer doesn't delete it speculatively and break some unknown caller.
title: SecretStore impl on AwsKmsBackend is unreachable through registry dispatch
